reasoning: The informal statement asserts that -4 is the greatest negative element of B, which requires: (a) -4 ∈ B, (b) -4 < 0, and (c) for all b ∈ B with b < 0, b ≤ -4. The only non-axiom theorem in the Lean file is `neg_four_in_B : -4 ∈ B`, which only establishes part (a). It does not encode the maximality/greatest-negative property, so the Lean statement is strictly weaker: it is implied by the informal claim but does not imply it (one could have -4 ∈ B while, say, -3 or -2 were also in B and would be "greater negative" elements). The auxiliary axioms are not the theorem being proved, and in any case they do not express the "greatest negative element" claim either.
